# Problems

::: {#exr-1}
## 1 point

A variable that takes on the values of 0 or 1 and is used to incorporate the effect of categorical variables in a regression model is called
\mc{
\item a constant variable  
\item an interaction   
\item a dummy variable     
\item None of these alternatives is correct.
}
:::


::: {#exr-2}

\mcc[2]{
\item a constant variable  
\item an interaction   
\item a dummy variable     
\item None of these alternatives is correct.
}

:::


::: {.cell}

:::




::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}


| XXX $x$ | YYY $y$ |
|:-------:|:-------:|
|    1    |    3    |
|    2    |    4    |
|    3    |    3    |


:::
:::





::: {.answer}
1. $y=\beta_0+\beta_1x_1+\beta_2x_2+\beta_3x_1x_2+\beta_4x_1^2+\beta_5x_2^2+\varepsilon$
2. $y=\beta_0+\beta_1x_1+\beta_2x_2+\beta_3x_1x_2+\varepsilon$
:::



::: {.content-hidden when-meta="show_solution"}
{{< pagebreak >}} 
:::




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
fit <- lm(y~x, data=dat)
s <- capture.output(summary(fit))
```
:::



\begin{alltt}

Call:
lm(formula = y ~ x, data = dat)

Residuals:
      1       2       3 
-0.3333  0.6667 -0.3333 

Coefficients:
              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
(Intercept)  3.333e+00  1.247e+00   2.673    0.228
x           -7.850e-16  5.774e-01   0.000    1.000

Residual standard error: 0.8165 on 1 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  \red{1. _______},	Adjusted R-squared:     -1 
F-statistic: 8.875e-31 on 1 and 1 DF,  p-value: 1

\end{alltt}




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
my_panel <- function(x, y, ...) {
    panel.smooth(x, y, col.smooth = "#BC4B00", ...)
}
plot(fit, which = 5, panel = my_panel, cook.col = "black", cook.lty = 2)
```
